title: Release helper stays maintainer-only
summary: The npm release helper is source repo maintainer tooling, not runtime package functionality. Keep public package metadata free of release:npm scripts, and exclude dist/release.js from the npm tarball. Maintainers can bui
body:
The npm release helper is source-repo maintainer tooling, not runtime package functionality. Keep public package metadata free of release:npm scripts, and exclude dist/release.js from the npm tarball. Maintainers can build the repo and run node mcp/dist/release.js from a checkout when publishing; installed users should only see the kage and kage-graph-mcp runtime commands.
Evidence: User questioned why the npm publish command was part of the package after 1.1.15; 1.1.16 removes the public npm scripts and adds !dist/release.js to package files.
Verified by: npm test --prefix mcp; npm --cache /private/tmp/kage-npm-cache pack --dry-run
